note: Sharded carousel: docs split across workers, a query rides ALL shards in parallel (revolution = N/8), partial heaps merged. Cuts the latency FLOOR 8x: p50 4.3ms @ rate 100 (vs per-worker carousel 77ms, per-query 12ms) - it's dynamic intra-query parallelism (021). BUT lower capacity: cliffs at ~550-600 QPS (1052ms @600). More seats does NOT help (compute/popcount-bound at saturation: 751 throughput flat for seats 8->128, latency rises 2790->3363ms). So spreading each query across all cores minimizes latency but limits cross-query concurrency -> lower throughput. Opposite sweet spot to the per-worker carousel (039: floor 30-77ms, capacity >800).
